skill.name: senior-architect
skill.description: Design and review software architecture for web, mobile, backend, data, and cloud systems. Provides architecture workflows, trade-off guidance, diagram planning, and lightweight local analysis scripts.
skill.summary: Plan scalable systems, compare technology choices, and document architecture decisions.
skill.author: alirezarezvani

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found the weak cryptography, reconnaissance, environment access, and shell execution alerts are false positives from prose, fenced examples, or command documentation. The skill includes local Python scripts that can write JSON output to a user-supplied path, so it should be published with a low-risk filesystem notice.

low_findings.title: User-Controlled Output File Write
low_findings.description: The three bundled Python scripts accept an output path and write JSON results when the JSON option is used. This is an explicit local report feature, but it can overwrite files that the running process can access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file write is direct and uses a user-supplied path. Confidence is limited to low severity because it is an explicit CLI output option with no network transfer or hidden execution.

low_findings.title: Documented Commands Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The shell-command alerts point to fenced documentation examples for running Python, npm, Docker, kubectl, and setup commands. No code path invokes a shell, interpolates user input into commands, or uses subprocess APIs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged content is inside Markdown code fences and is user-visible usage documentation. The reviewed Python scripts contain no shell execution calls.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography and Reconnaissance Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak-cryptography detections occur in descriptive text, Markdown headings, or argparse descriptions, not cryptographic APIs. The reconnaissance alerts point to generic reference sections and do not enumerate host, network, or credential data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no md5, sha1, cipher, host discovery, or credential collection code. The scanner appears to have matched substrings in ordinary documentation and tool names.

low_findings.title: Environment File Alert Is Documentation Only
low_findings.description: The environment-file alert points to a setup example that copies a sample environment file. No script reads .env files, environment variables, or secrets.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The only environment-file reference is a visible setup command in Markdown. No runtime code accesses environment variables or transmits secrets.

dangerous_patterns.title: Optional JSON Report Write
dangerous_patterns.description: Each script can write JSON output to the path passed with the output option. Users should run it only in trusted workspaces and avoid privileged paths.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The write behavior is clear in all three scripts and uses the CLI output argument. It is not hidden or malicious, so the pattern is low severity.

content.value_statement: Architecture decisions often lack clear trade-offs, diagrams, and review structure. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code produce practical architecture plans and decision records.
actual_capabilities: Guides architecture planning for React, Next.js, Node.js, Python, Go, mobile, database, and cloud systems.
Structures trade-off analysis for technology choices, integration patterns, scalability, and maintainability.
Provides reference workflows for system design, architecture patterns, and technical decision review.
Includes local Python scripts that validate a target path and produce basic JSON analysis reports.
Supports prompts for architecture diagrams, dependency review, and decision documentation.
limitations: The bundled scripts are placeholders and do not perform deep dependency or architecture discovery.
Architecture recommendations still require project-specific requirements, constraints, and human review.
It does not deploy infrastructure, modify application code, or verify production performance.
It does not replace formal threat modeling, compliance review, or security testing.

use_cases.title: Plan a New Product Architecture
use_cases.description: Create a practical system plan with components, data flows, risks, and technology trade-offs.

use_cases.title: Review an Existing System
use_cases.description: Assess maintainability, scalability, dependency boundaries, and integration risks before a major change.

use_cases.title: Document Architecture Decisions
use_cases.description: Turn technical discussions into clear decision records with context, options, consequences, and next steps.

prompt_templates.title: Beginner Architecture Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when starting a small application or feature area.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a simple architecture for this product idea: [describe product]. Include main components, data storage, APIs, deployment approach, and key risks.

prompt_templates.title: Technology Trade-Off Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when choosing between competing technologies.
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare these technology options for my system: [options]. Evaluate scalability, maintainability, team fit, security, cost, and migration risk.

prompt_templates.title: System Modernization Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when improving an existing service or monolith.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this current architecture: [describe system]. Propose a phased modernization plan with dependencies, risks, validation steps, and rollback points.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Architecture Review Board
prompt_templates.scenario: Use before committing to a high-impact design.
prompt_templates.prompt: Act as an architecture review board for this proposal: [paste proposal]. Challenge assumptions, identify failure modes, assess operational impact, and produce a decision recommendation.

output_examples.input: Design an architecture for a customer support platform with real-time chat and analytics.
output: A component map covering web app, API gateway, chat service, event stream, analytics store, and admin tools.
Trade-offs between managed messaging, self-hosted queues, relational storage, and warehouse-backed reporting.
Operational risks covering message ordering, retention, tenant isolation, alerting, and cost growth.

output_examples.input: Compare GraphQL and REST for a mobile and web product team.
output: A decision matrix that weighs client flexibility, caching, observability, schema governance, and backend complexity.
A recommendation based on team size, release cadence, data shape, and long-term platform ownership.

output_examples.input: Review a monolith that needs to support regional scaling.
output: A phased plan that separates read-heavy paths, introduces service boundaries where needed, and preserves rollback options.
A list of risks for data consistency, migration sequencing, deployment ownership, and monitoring gaps.

best_practices: Provide concrete requirements, traffic assumptions, compliance needs, and team constraints before asking for architecture advice.
Ask for trade-offs and failure modes, not only a preferred technology stack.
Convert final recommendations into decision records with owners, review dates, and measurable validation steps.
anti_patterns: Do not accept a broad architecture recommendation without project-specific constraints and evidence.
Do not use the placeholder scripts as a substitute for real static analysis or dependency scanning.
Do not ask the skill to apply production changes without separate review, tests, and deployment controls.

faq.question: Can this skill create architecture diagrams?
faq.answer: It can plan diagram structure and component relationships. The included script is a basic placeholder and not a full diagram renderer.

faq.question: Does it support both web and mobile systems?
faq.answer: Yes. The guidance covers web, backend, mobile, database, API, and cloud architecture topics.

faq.question: Can it choose a technology stack for me?
faq.answer: It can compare options and recommend a direction when you provide requirements, constraints, and team context.

faq.question: Does it modify my project automatically?
faq.answer: No. The scripts validate paths and can write basic reports, but they do not change application source code.

faq.question: Is this a security review tool?
faq.answer: No. It includes security considerations, but it does not replace threat modeling, penetration testing, or compliance review.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
